title: Luminex Bead Conjugation
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"7vnnc","text":"Protocol for amine-coupling of proteins to magnetic Luminex beads","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5e5t3","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"5kt77","text":"Quantity as written: 1 x 106 beads under typical yields (sufficient for 2000 wells assuming 500 beads/well)","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"sup","offset":27,"length":1}],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"595am","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]},{"key":"8fvsm","text":"### Needs a good qualification section!","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":[]}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Resuspend beads by briefly vortexing; dispense 100 µL, (contains approx. 1.25 million beads)
BEAD SEPARATION BLOCK
Collect volume by pulse centrifugation 00:00:03

Place on magnetic tube rack, shielded from light 00:01:00 Protocol tip Partially open the microcentrifuge tube before placing in the rack. This allows them to be opened on-rack with minimal disruption of the bead pellet that's held in place only by the magnet.

Remove 90 µL of supernatant (while still on-rack) using a pipette n.b. This leaves a 10 µL excess that allows the removal of supernatant at all subsequent steps to use the same volume that was added immediately prior.
WASH BLOCK
Resuspend in 100 µL 1x PBS; vortex briefly to mix
Collect volume by pulse centrifugation 00:00:03
Place on magnetic tube rack, shielded from light 00:01:00
Remove supernatant using a pipette

Resuspend beads in a solution containing: - 100 millimolar (mM) sodium phosphate (pH 6.2) 80 µL - 12.5 mg/mL (approx. 65 millimolar (mM) ) in sodium phosphate (pH 6.2) 5 µL - 12.5 mg/mL (approx. 58 millimolar (mM) ) sulfo-NHS in phosphate (pH 6.2) 5 µL EDC (no-weigh formulation) Thermo Scientific Catalog #A35391 sulfo-NHS (no-weigh format) Thermo Scientific Catalog #A39269
Mix tubes end-over-end, shielded from light Room temperature 00:20:00
go to step #2 (separation block) for removal of supernatant
Resuspend beads in 25 µg/mL protein of interest 250 µL , made in 1x PBS
Mix tubes end-over-end, shielded from light Room temperature 02:00:00

go to step #3 (wash block) for a total of 3 washes using 400 µL 1x PBS-TBN n.b. DO NOT use P1000 for removing supernatant as the wider tip and faster flow tends to lead to greater bead loss. Instead, use a P200 twice. PBS-TBN Teknova Catalog #P0210
Perform final resuspension in 250 µL 1x PBS-TBN PBS-TBN Teknova Catalog #P0210
Will the beads be used immediately?
Dilute beads by 1:10 and 1:100 in 1x PBS
Measure concentration of each dilution by cell counter Typical yields are appox. 1 x 10 6 beads, or 4 x 10 6 beads/mL
creator: Andrew Crowley
